channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Humpback whale breaks migration record with 15,000 kilometer ocean journey
item.description: Scientists have discovered a new chapter in humpback whale migration: two whales traveled between breeding grounds in Australia and...

item.title: Scientists use DNA from poop to save the worldâs rarest marsupial
item.description: Scientists in Australia are using advanced DNA techniques to help save the critically endangered Gilbertâs potoroo, one of the worldâs rarest marsupials.

item.title: Rainforest discovery challenges long-held views on early human migration
item.description: Scientists previously thought ancient humans avoided rainforests, but new evidence suggests they lived there 150,000 years ago.

item.title: T. rex's small arms may have evolved due to its massive head
item.description: Researchers propose that T. rex's tiny arms were a result of its oversized head becoming the primary weapon for hunting.
